If you're trekking towards Limansk, could you grab about five leathers? Mikhalych will make it worth your while. Just make it quick, okay? Two days max.
Flesh hides from the hunter for the guys at the Warehouses Shinkar at the Army Warehouses is waiting for ten tanned flesh hides. I can make it in a couple of days. The folks at the Army Warehouses are very interested in flesh hides. Well, I had a small stash, so I promised them. If you're heading that way - could you grab ten pieces? Think you can manage it in a couple of days?
Return the debt Baldy is asking to talk to a stalker from Freedom, and, if possible, return the debt. Gives a day for everything. For a while I was hanging around the Warehouses area, well, the guys and I played some cards a few times. One of them got really unlucky - blew everything and then some, just on his word of honor. Well, generally speaking, I'm not a vindictive guy. I figured, well, it happens. But the guy turned out to have a conscience. Sent word recently that he scraped together some loot and is ready to pay back the debt. It's just that they aren't exactly loved around here, Freedom members I mean. If you aren't too busy, I'd ask you to run over there and get back what he owes. Truth is, a couple of stalkers already went that way recently, I asked them about this too, but I think they won't be in a hurry - they were going to gather loot. Well? Are you in? Find our 'conscientious' friend and help him return the debt? I think you can get it done in a day. Whoever wanted to help me with the debtor - no need anymore. In short, no need to look for the debtor anymore. Sorted it out.
What are you staring at, kid? You gonna ask questions or are you just passing through? What's your name, curious? I'm Marked One. I have a question. Or rather, an offer. Yeah? And what kind of offer is that? Drink a half-liter to get acquainted? Then I'm all for it! Something like that. Baldy, the one in the Bar who likes to gamble, told me you wanted to pay back your debt, but just can't go there yourself? Ah! You're here about that business. Yeah, we were sitting together once, and the devil made me bet so damn much. Thought I'd win it back, but no, didn't work out. I won't say anything bad about Baldy, and nobody else will either. He knows how to play, you could say he's an ace at it. And whoever loses is always unhappy... Hmm... Could we get to the point? What do I need to pass on? I'm in a hurry. Well, I've got a debt hanging over me, three and a half grand in greenbacks. I gathered some loot, traded it with Sidor for checks. Except, I'm completely out of rubles, can't even afford to buy water. Let's do this: you spot me thirty-five grand, and I'll pass four checks through you. And Baldy will give you the difference. Shake on it? Hmm. I don't have that much in my account right now. Hold on. Alright, shake on it. Here's thirty-five. Well, suit yourself, maybe someone else will agree. Alright! It's a deal. Here are the checks. Just don't disappear on me, otherwise you know yourself, you never know what might happen later... I'm not like that. Well, see ya... Ah! Hello, Marked One... Well, are you heading to the bar? Will you take the debt to Baldy with you? Seems like it. Did you ask for a tenner? Or what? No, what, did you forget? Thirty-five grand. Rubles. That's, consequently, the difference between four tons of bucks and my debt. U-u-uh... I don't have that much on me right now. Hello, Marked One, what are you doing here? Thought you wanted to quickly pass my debt to Baldy? Or what?
Everything is fine. I met with him. How is he over there? Giving it all back? Come on, speak plainly. Here, he passed on four checks. Wow! Didn't expect that. Well... There are still honest people left in the Zone. Even two of them. If we count you. It's almost embarrassing... Let's count up to three honest ones - he said he owed less than four grand. I sponsored him, a whole thirty-five grand, even if it was rubles, it was my very own hard-earned cash! So... You owe me a reward!
Find out about the debtor Baldy is asking to talk to a stalker, and find out about his friend, the debtor. Gives five days to do it. A stalker lost a large sum of money to me. But, as he said, his PDA was damaged at the time so he couldn't transfer the losses immediately. Well, I trust people, and he was a pretty well-known guy, Vadim Flint, ever heard of him? No? Well, I believed him. I don't know if I was wrong to or not, but it's been two weeks and this Flint hasn't been seen at our Bar. And recently it became known that his partner showed up. Could you go and find out what's what? And can I hope to get the debt back? Will you agree to talk to this partner? Whoever wanted to help me with the debtor - no need anymore. In short, no need to look for the debtor anymore. Sorted it out.
Hello, friend. Let's shake on it? What's your name? I'm Marked One. I have something to discuss with you. Well then, let's talk, I'm never opposed to a good, friendly chat with a nice guy. I was at the Bar the other day, and a regular there, Baldy is his name, asked me to inquire about your partner, who goes by the name Flint. Yeah? Running errands for that cardsharp? Aren't you ashamed? Okay, okay, don't get worked up... I'm just saying. And Vadim is lying in a hospital right now. Had an unlucky encounter with a chimera, almost lost his leg, so it seems he won't be returning to the Zone anymore. Won't be returning? Alright, then that's what I'll tell Baldy.. Hold on a second. Vadim is a real man. And he surely wouldn't want a bad reputation left behind him. I know about that debt to Baldy. I think we should return it and put an end to this. Truth is, things aren't too great for me right now either, but...\nLet's do this. If you're at Yantar, go to Sakharov, do you know him? Right. Vadim left two capsules with him to be opened, have him give them to you, and you settle up with Baldy? Alright? Oh, almost forgot, Sakharov needs payment for opening the capsules. From Sakharov? That means giving up two good artifacts for two capsules... Fine, if I'm at Yantar - I'll tell him... Ah! Hello, Marked One... Well, how's it going? Alive and well? Fair enough.
I'm here on behalf of a stalker named Flint, for the arts from the capsules. What did you say? Flint? Just a minute. Ah! 'Vadim Flint'... Yes, there was such a person, according to the records - he left two capsules. Do you want to take the artifacts from these capsules? Hmm... This Flint did not pay for the opening... You know the rates - a level two artifact... Yes. Fine. One will be enough for both opened capsules. Do you have it? Yes, please, here's the artifact. Hmm, I don't have it on me. I'll go get it from storage. Well, well... Here, take the artifacts, from the looks of it, they are practically identical, you could say - identical twins. Which leads to the thought: how did he manage to find such capsules. If it's not too much trouble - look into this matter... Well, that's unlikely, Flint won't be coming back to the Zone. A pity, a great pity... It would be highly desirable to research the phenomenon.
Well. Not only did I find your friend, I did more. Did more? What's that supposed to mean? Did you kill him or something? Don't be crazy! I just found this Flint's loot, which he wanted to give for the debt. Here, two very good artifacts. Well damn! You've got skills! I kinda didn't even expect it. Already wrote it all off... Well then, I need to reward you... Probably. Probably? I trekked across half the Zone at your request! No 'probably' about it! You owe me a mountain of loot or cash!
Confirm debt write-off Tell the stalker that Baldy agrees to write off the debt in exchange for the gear the stalker got from the military. Three days should be enough for persuasion. A little bird told me that some stalkers managed to fleece the military a bit at Agroprom. Supposedly a lot of new military gear for the Zone fell into their hands. I hope my interest is fairly obvious? Anyway, it just so happens that one of those who got their hands on this blessed freebie owes me a small debt. If you agree to pass on that I'm willing to write off everything he owes me in exchange for some of what he got, I'd be very grateful! Well? Do you agree to find the 'lucky guy'? Whoever wanted to help me with the debtor - no need anymore. In short, no need to look for the debtor anymore. Sorted it out.
Oh! Broski! Hello! How's life? Shake on it! What's your name then? Call me Marked One. I'm here on an errand for you. Interesting, interesting, what kind of 'errand'? Maybe someone sent a half-liter? Because I, uh, accept such 'errands' with pleasure! Something like that. You know Baldy? He says he can write off all your debts. What? That's kinda... For Baldy not to wring every penny out! Nobody remembers anything like that. Are you sure you're not getting things mixed up? Are we talking about the same Baldy? The godless cardsharp? Yeah, that's him. He said that for a part of the goods you took from the military, he'll write off the entire debt. A-a-ah... Well, that makes sense then. I thought Baldy had become human, thought maybe now the blind dogs will cheerfully wag their little tails and walk on their hind legs... But that's what's going on!\nHow much is that debt anyway, spit and rub it out. What about you, aren't you heading to the Bar? I'll give this bloodsucker a new military suit, against the debt. Except one like this is worth more than three of my debts. Let's do this: you seem like an honest guy and all that, but... In short, you give me three hundred rounds for an AK, I give you the armor, you take it to Baldy, and settle up with him. Shake on it? I'll think about it, three hundred rounds don't just grow on trees. It's a deal. Here's the ammo. Well think, think, maybe I'll get ready to drop by the Bar myself one of these days. So... Oh! Shake on it once more! See ya, 'guarantor'... Well, see ya... Jokester... Oh! Hello, Marked One... Still working for Baldy? Uh-huh, kinda. Remind me what you wanted? Well, what, did you forget? Three hundred rounds for an AK and all business between us will be successfully concluded. Three hundred... Right, I remember. I think I'll be going now. Hello, Marked One, what are you doing here? Thought you wanted to run to Baldy? Or what?
Passed on your terms. And so? Did he agree? When will he be at the bar? Didn't say? Was planning to in the next few days. Here, he passed on a new military armor, to pay off his debts. Armor... Thought it would be something else. Alright, everything's fine. See ya, friend Marked One. What? What do you mean 'see ya'?! You owe me what you promised according to our deal!
Find out the debtor's fate Find one of the hunters and buy information from him about the fate of the presumably alive debtor. Gotta get it done in three days. So here's the deal... There was this very lucky stalker, Vitya Ferret, heard of him? No? Well okay. Anyway, this same Ferret owed another stalker a lot of loot. Well, and this other one, goes by the nickname Whistler, lost a hefty sum to me against that debt.\nA couple of days later word got out that both Ferret and Whistler died in a clash with a gang of punks. Seems like business as usual, but... A few days ago, one of the hunters came to me and reported that there's a guy willing to sell information about the current whereabouts of these two 'corpses'. The guy himself is afraid to be seen in contact with me, so he's asking to send a middleman.\nWill you be this middleman? Will you find this guy? Whoever wanted to help me with the debtor - no need anymore. In short, no need to look for the debtor anymore. Sorted it out.
What? You're looking at me like I killed your brother. My name is Marked One, I'm here on business. Well then. Let's get acquainted. And what kind of business? I'm from Baldy. He says you have something for him? Hmm... From him then... I have a photo. It shows the ones he's interested in at a place everyone knows. I'm willing to sell this photograph. For a symbolic fee. And just how 'symbolic' is this fee? Judging by what I heard about the person of interest's losses, the fee will be one-tenth. In units more understandable to outsiders, I want a thousand dollars. In cash or, in an extreme case, by check. So come on, settle up right here and we'll go our separate ways. I didn't think I'd need it... Don't have it on me. Alright, here's the check. Let's have what you got. You should have thought ahead... Go find it. Here's the photograph. They're all there. Baldy knows who and what. Uh-huh. We'll see what Baldy says. You? Well? Are you ready? Wouldn't agree to a smaller sum? I said - the sum is a tenth of it. A normal cut for a tip. Any less - there's no reason for me to risk my neck. A thousand! Hmm, a pity. Don't have that much on me. Look here, let's do this - you don't know me, and I don't know you. We part ways like ships at sea!
I got the photograph. Where these live debtors of yours are. Damn it! What kind of people... Always have to look for them, scare them, can't just return what's owed... Where's the photo? Here. By the way, I shelled out my own hard-earned cash for it... What? Yes, yes, of course. Here's something for vodka for you... Don't you try to pull the wool over my eyes! I shelled out a grand of bucks! What the hell do you mean, 'for vodka'!
Find out about cat protection Find a stalker, and try to find out from him about an artifact that scares off cats. Three days for asking around should be enough. A stalker came into the bar recently, Kostyan Belomorkanal, who let slip on 'this' matter that his friend knows a recipe that allows you to make weapons practically eternal. Well, no wear and tear, no damage, no need to clean or lubricate... You get it? Might be just talk of course, but, how do we check it? Will you go to this 'friend'? People. Regarding the recipe for guns, no need for anything. People, about the case with the gun recipe. It's a fake basically. Standing down on this for now.
Hello, buddy. Did we get into a fight a month ago? Or not? Am I mixing things up? Pintle? Right? Hmm, no. Not with me. I'm Marked One. Marked One? Seems like I've heard that moniker... Something connected to the showdowns between stalkers and the bros? No? Well, whatever, what did you want, buddy? Do you know Belomorkanal? Belomor? You mean Kostyan? Well yeah. We went on a raid together a couple of times. Didn't rake in a lot of dough, but we didn't leave empty-handed either. And what's up with him? Well everything seems fine for now. Here's the situation - he said at the Bar that you know some recipe that strengthens weapons? Huh? Well, and he calls himself a buddy. I told him in secret, and he goes and does this! Well yeah. I know a thing or two about it. And I can even tell a curious and not greedy stalker. For just one artifact. No lower than level two. Well? Want to learn such a recipe? A level two art just for words? I'll think about it. U-u-uh... Alright, here's the art. Write down the recipe. Well go on, think about it. I'm in no rush... Write? There's nothing to write there. Memorize this...\nIn the Swamps, the eggheads found some tricky way to mix up something from chimera claws that strengthens metal. Only it doesn't seem to last very long and doesn't strengthen it all that much. Holy smokes, giving up an art for such garbage! Fine. He'll owe me big time. Long time no see, buddy! Still interested? Well yeah, I'm interested. Will you tell me the recipe? Well we already discussed this - you give me a level two art, and I'll tell you everything I know. Let's shake on it, friend Marked One. Still interested in recipes? No? Well, suit yourself.
I found that 'friend' with the recipe for durable guns, talked to him. Oh! Great. And so? Is there a recipe? Ingredients? Hmm... There's one ingredient. Or rather two: the nerds at the Swamps and chimera claws... Total garbage, basically. Ah! So he was blabbing about those scientist gimmicks! Well, almost everyone already knows about that. Seems like the duration is short there and... In general - a fake, just like I thought... I gave him a good artifact for this fake. I kinda need to be compensated for my expenses!
Find out about a new recipe Find a stalker, and try to get the recipe from him for 'collapsing' a Merry-go-round anomaly. Three days for the search. Rumor has it there is an artifact that can remove, or, as the eggheads say - 'collapse' a Merry-go-round anomaly. Not bad, right? There's a kid who, according to intel, knows the recipe for this artifact. What about you? Will you take the job to find this kid and find out the details? Just needs to be fast. Can you get it done in about three days? No need to find out about the recipe for now. Maybe later. People. The recipe for removing the Merry-go-round is known. You can come see me.
Well? And what? Are we just going to keep staring? Who are you? Well... Marked One. Marked One? Heard something about a guy like that. Supposedly you hauled stiffs, right? No, didn't happen. I think. Anyway, I have another question... Didn't haul them? Strange, but people said that... Alright, looks like they were lying. What's the question then? Will you share the recipe - how to remove anomalies? Share? This is the Zone, brother! You won't even get a pimple for free. If you want to know what I know - pay up! An artifact. Level two. Then we'll have a conversation. A level two art? Hmm... Don't have one on me. U-u-uh... Alright, here's the art. Write down the recipe. Well when you have it, then we'll talk. Write? There's nothing to write there. Memorize this...\nThere is an artifact, it's called a Spring - very expensive! You need to boil it in a Snowflake, and it'll turn red. That's exactly what they call it - a Ruby Spring. And if you toss this same 'ruby' into a Merry-go-round, not even a memory of the anomaly will be left. Except for the artifact too... A pity. Hmm. Alright, I got it all... Ah! It's you, driver. Collecting stiffs? Hey, knock it off. Still selling the recipe? Well we already discussed this - you give me a level two art, and I'll tell you everything I know. Let's shake on it, Marked One. Still interested in recipes? No? Well, suit yourself.
So I guess I know how to eliminate a Merry-go-round now... Well, well. And how is that? You need to boil a Spring artifact in a Snowflake anomaly. That's the recipe, basically. Right... Uh-huh. Understood. Wasting an artifact on such a whim... Who could I pawn it off on... What do you want? I gave up a good artifact for this recipe. I kinda need to be compensated for my expenses!
Find out about a new recipe Find a stalker, and try to get the recipe from him for 'collapsing' a Vortex anomaly. Deadline is three days. There is almost one hundred percent certain information that there is an artifact that can remove, or, as the eggheads say - 'collapse' a Vortex anomaly. Such information might interest a certain someone very much. In this case - me. As I was told here, one of the hunters was bragging that he knows everything about this. Will you find this beast-catcher? No need to find out about the recipe for now. Maybe later. People. Found the recipe for removing the Vortex. You can come see me.
Hello, stalker. Wow, you got hit hard! What's your name? Well... I'm Marked One. Marked One? I've heard something about you... Ah! You fleeced Sidor? You're the man, somebody should have dragged that mug of his across the table a long time ago... Attaboy! That's bullshit... Anyway, I have another question... Didn't haul them? Strange, but people said that... Alright, means it wasn't you apparently. What's the question then? Will you share the recipe - how to remove anomalies? Share? This is the Zone, brother! You won't even get a pimple for free. If you want to know what I know - pay up! An artifact. Level two. Then we'll have a conversation. A level two art? Hmm... Don't have one on me. U-u-uh... Alright, here's the art. Write down the recipe. Well when you have it, then we'll talk. Write? There's nothing to write there. Memorize this...\nThere is an artifact, it's called a Battery - very expensive! Now if you first boil it in a Snowflake, then it will turn red. That's exactly what they call it - a Ruby Battery. And if you toss this same 'ruby' into a Vortex or an Electro, not even a memory of them will be left. Except for the artifact too... A pity. Hmm. Alright, I got it all... Ah! It's you, Marked One. Want to whack Sidor? No? Your loss... Hey, knock it off. Still selling the recipe? Well we already discussed this - you give me a level two art, and I'll tell you everything I know. Let's shake on it, Marked One. Still interested in recipes? No? Well, suit yourself.
I found out about how to blow away Vortexes... Well, well. And how is that? You need to boil a Battery artifact in a Snowflake anomaly. That's the whole recipe, basically. Right... Uh-huh. Understood. Wasting an artifact on such a whim... Who could I pawn it off on... What do you want? I gave up a good artifact for this recipe. I kinda need to be compensated for my expenses!
Find out about a new recipe Find a stalker, and try to get the recipe from him for 'collapsing' a Springboard anomaly. Deadline is three days. As far as I know - there is an artifact that can remove, or, as the eggheads say - 'collapse' a Springboard anomaly. They say that one of the experienced stalkers knows the recipe for this artifact. What about you? Will you take the job to find out the details? No need to find out about the recipe for now. Maybe later. People. The recipe for removing the Springboard is known. You can come see me.
What do you want? Why so hostile? Shall we get acquainted? My name is Marked One. What do I care what your name is? Did you want something or are you just here to chat? Well... Alright, I have a question for you... Everyone wants something, walking around, asking... What's the question? Will you share the recipe - how to remove anomalies? Wha-a-at!? What law says I have to share anything with you? If you want to know what I know - pay up. With artifacts. An artifact. One will be enough this time. Level two. A level two art? Hmm... Don't have one on me. U-u-uh... Alright, here's the art. Write down the recipe. Well when you have it, then we'll talk. Your artifact is complete shit! Write down the recipe for you? Memorize this... \nA Springboard anomaly can be removed from the road with an artifact boiled from a Pellicle. Know about that one? It's pale green. Now, if you first boil it in a Snowflake, it will turn red. And if you toss this boiled art into a Springboard, not even a memory of the anomaly will be left. Got it? Or should I repeat it? Hmm. Alright, I got it all... You again? What is it this time? About the recipe, I... Well we already discussed this - you give me a level two art, and I'll tell you everything I know. Ah, you again, Marked One. Still interested in recipes? No? Good.
Well, I found out how to get rid of a Springboard... Well, well. And how is that? You need to boil a Pellicle artifact in a Snowflake anomaly. That's the whole recipe, basically. Right... Uh-huh. Understood. Wasting an artifact on such a whim... Who could I pawn it off on... What do you want? I gave up a good artifact for this recipe. I kinda need to be compensated for my expenses!
Find out about a new recipe Find one of the researchers, and try to get the recipe from him for birthing an anomaly. Deadline is three days. Not too long ago there was a Duty member here who said there's a way to create, or as he put it - 'birth' an anomaly anywhere. This might be interesting for someone. And therefore it would be highly desirable to find out the exact recipe. I asked around and found out that maybe the nerds know about this trick. There's one specific egghead who can be coaxed into this. Will you make a run? To this nerd? No need to find out about the anomaly appearance method for now. Maybe later. People. We have information on how to get an anomaly... You can come see me.
Good day. Shall we get acquainted? Let's, I'm Marked One. Haven't you heard? Marked One? Hmm, no, I don't recall. You'll have to excuse me, these stalker nicknames are somewhat... uniform... And what question brings you to us? Just a small matter. They say you know a lot about anomalies? Is it even possible to know a lot about anomalies? We can't even understand the nature of these formations! For example - the widely known Electro anomaly, it has absolutely no relation to electromagnetic energy! We do not register any properties that should be observed during the discharges of these lightning bolts, which look so similar to electrical ones! Or the so-called Slime!... Stop stop... I understood that I didn't understand anything... They say there's a possibility to create, or, let's say, 'birth' an anomaly in a certain place? Haven't heard of such a thing? 'Birth' an anomaly? Probably more like 'sprout'? We are increasingly leaning towards the idea that anomalies are closer to plants. And the artifacts that stalkers find here, in the Zone, are the seeds of these 'plants'. Then, if the conditions are created, one can 'sprout' an anomaly from a 'seed', an artifact. From what I've heard, there were successful attempts with an artifact of the 'electrical' group. Unfortunately, I don't have exact data, but it came up once in a conversation with a colleague. And he said that one of the stalkers wrote down this information for sale. But... They couldn't agree on a price. And the next day the stalker disappeared near the Tuzla bridge. That's exactly what he said - 'Tuzla'... Yeah, a pity... I was hoping... C'est la vie, as the French say. There are many secrets in this anomalous Zone. One more, one less... Try to find the poor wretch, or at least what's left of him... Well yeah, makes sense. Tuzla... Yes? Did we not finish our conversation? He died, apparently, that stalker who knew about how to 'grow' an anomaly... Hello.
About the 'birthing' of the anomaly. Yes? And? You need to boil an artifact in a Snowflake anomaly... Here, the recipe is written down, basically. True, the guy who wrote it has been growing anomalies in another world for a long time now... Right... You didn't whack the poor bastard, did you? I haven't noticed such tendencies in you. Although - another man's soul is a mystery... The recipe will come in handy. Well what else? I had to do some serious running around for this piece of paper... I'm expecting compensation for my wasted time, so to speak.
Find out the method of protection against bloodsuckers Find a stalker, and try to get the method of protection against bloodsuckers from him. Deadline is three days. Have you ever encountered bloodsuckers? Well, you've probably been lucky so far? Those bastards produce something that makes a person's skin burst. Terrible stuff. But there's a rumor that someone found a way to fight against this bloodsucker 'radiation'. Supposedly you can find an artifact that protects you very well. I have my suspicions about one of these 'experts'. Would you agree to pay him a visit and feel him out regarding this miracle artifact? I think it'll be useful for everyone. No need to find out about the artifact against bloodsuckers for now. Maybe later. People, we already know which artifact protects against bloodsuckers! You can come see me.
Ah, well? What's your name then? Well... Marked One. Marked One? Heard somethin' about a guy like that. Are you the one who squeezed Mole? 'Bout time somebody did - sneakin' around everywhere, botherin' everyone. Mole? Squeezed? No, everything's fine. Anyway, I have another question... So, wait, didn't they whack him? Or... Weird, people were sayin' that... Alright, looks like they were bullshittin'. What's the question? Someone said you've been having good luck with bloodsuckers? That you know some secret on how to survive their roar? Maybe you'll tell me? Well you should tell this 'somebody' that you won't even get a pimple for free, as they say. You wanna know my secret - pay up. Bucks from you. Two grand. Then we'll have a business talk. Two grand? In checks? Don't have it on me, but... I'll think about it. You take checks? Here, for two grand. Checks'll do. Nobody's complained so far... Think 'bout it. When you have it, then we'll talk. I'm takin' it, I'm takin' it. Respect. Listen here, what and how you gotta do when a bloodsucker shows up on the horizon... Well besides grabbin' your legs and runnin', obviously...\nSo here's the deal: there's this artifact - Balloon it's called? Findin' it is extremely hard, very rare. They say it's only been seen in the Rusty Forest, the one on the Radar. Activate this art on your belt and walk around whistlin'... the bloodsucker's pipe barely touches you... How am I supposed to score that? Alright... Ah! It's you, squeezer. Huntin' bloodsuckers? Or are they huntin' you? Yeah, them... What about you, haven't changed your mind about sharing your good fortune? Bro, we already chopped it up - you give me two grand greenbacks, and I tell you everything I know. Let's shake on it, Marked One. How's your huntin' goin'? Whacked a lot of bloodsuckers? Fair enough.
I found out about that secret artifact... Well, well. And, what kind of miracle is it? You need a very rare artifact called "Balloon". That's it, basically. Where to get it?.. He said it was found on the Radar, in the Rusty Forest. Right... Uh-huh... Understood. Someone was already talking about this, another scam for...\nWho could I pawn this info off on... Although, there's enough crazy people around, many will agree to buy... Could make a profit...\nListen, don't run your mouth too much about what you learned and heard, okay? I gave up serious cash for this information. So, if you want me to keep quiet, you owe me some good loot!
Find the deserter Find the stalker who left Duty and find out the reason for his departure. Gotta get it done in a couple of days. There is one delicate matter. One of our fighters, who was in good standing, suddenly left his post and departed in an unknown direction. We are not the army, of course, but we do not encourage desertion! I would like to find out what happened. And well, try to get an answer to the question - should we wait for this 'prodigal son' to return. If not - it wouldn't hurt to return the gear. The case of the missing comrade is canceled! The circumstances regarding our missing friend have been cleared up... Standing down on this for now.
Hello, friend. How's life? What's your name? I'm doing fine. My name is Marked One, haven't heard of me? How about yourself? Marked One? Heard something. They say you crawled into the underground and came out alive? Crazy... And I'm here, still alive for now... There was a rumor at the bar that you left Duty? Why is that? Didn't like the duds? The duds? What does that have to do with... My buddy got shot somewhere around these parts. Well, as soon as I found out, I hauled ass straight here. Thought I'd make it in time to help. Didn't make it. He died. Well, and I thought, what do we really need in life anyway? We fuss around, follow some kinds of rules. I'm sick of it! That's true, we do fuss around. Well, so, you're not planning on going back? No, I was thinking in my free time, I slogged away in this Duty for half a year, and I have absolutely nothing to show for it. I'll try to live like a stalker. I know the Zone a little already. I think I'll manage to save up a little for a rainy day. And I'll bail. Go home.\nYeah, by the way. Just to feel completely 'clean' I should return the gear I received for service. Are you heading to the Bar? Or maybe keep it for yourself. The armor, almost new, and my Abakan. If you don't mind sparing an art, I'll give it to you. A level three one, a good one. No, I'm not heading to the bar. Maybe later. Hmm. I have an art, let me take it. I'll give it to Petrenko. Level three you say... Wait a bit, I'll be quick. Alright then, suit yourself, I'll hold onto it for now. Then we have a deal. You're a good guy, even if you are Marked One. Here, take it. Well, well, watch it. I won't wait long. The gear is good, there'll be takers. Yeah, good. See ya. Ah! Hello, Marked One... Where are you heading? Heading to the bar, if you need to pass on what you wanted, here's the art. I'm off on business. And where business will lead me - who knows. Well! Alright... Let the old quartermaster rejoice. Here, take it. Uh-huh. Let him rejoice. Well, see ya! Hello bro! How's life friend? Everything fine? Well, suit yourself.
I met with that guy... He's not coming back. Not coming back, you say? Are you sure? And what actually happened? Why is that? How should I say this... Basically, he's tired of the Zone. Plus he lost a friend. So he decided to hit the road. Says he's going home. Asked me to return his harness here. So he wouldn't be considered a thief, so to speak. A pity. He was a good fighter. Not like some. And you too, I see, didn't let us down, could have hoarded it for yourself. I respect that. I gave him a good artifact for this deal. I kinda need to be compensated for my expenses!
Find one good jacket Nimble is asking to find his jacket with chainmail lining, which he tossed while fleeing from blind dogs near a fallen tree, close to the railway tunnel that leads to the Sanatorium. He needs the jacket in good condition, since it's meant to be sold. I had scored a pretty good jacket, swiped it from a bandit stash... Light, durable, with a chainmail lining... I already made a deal with a rookie in the village - he agreed to shell out a serious amount of cash for it. But when exiting the railway tunnel, you know, the one through which you can climb into the former hospital, a pack of dogs ambushed me - so I dropped everything I gathered... Maybe you could look for this outfit? It should be somewhere near the fallen tree, on that side of the embankment, not far from the tunnel... I'll make it worth your while...\nJust understand... today he still has the money, but tomorrow it'll all vanish somewhere and I might miss out. I think if you don't bring it in a couple of days, it'll be too late.\nIf it works out, I'll try to reward you properly. I have a stash for a rainy day: This thing... I don't need the assault rifle anymore. I resolved the issue. In short, I already have the assault rifle. A good guy helped out.
Search for a gun at the Army Warehouses Nimble needs a gun that's somewhere with the mercs at the Army Warehouses. Asking to find it. Got a couple of days... I was told that one of the guys, well they seem to be mercs, said that they got saddled with some new rifle. And supposedly, he wouldn't mind trading it for something else. Might you be at the Army Warehouses? I blurted out about this gun to someone in the heat of the moment, and well... In short, if I don't show it in a couple of days, things won't be too good for me... Could you help? Eh? You're such a lucky guy... Well, and I'll pay up, everything fair and square. This thing... I don't need the assault rifle anymore. I resolved the issue. In short, I already have the assault rifle. A good guy helped out.
Need to quiet down the boars Boars set up a lair right next to the place where Wolf and his friend made a stash. Wolf asked to clear out the lair quickly, in a couple of hours, promised to reward generously. Listen, Marked One, a friend and I had a stash here, right behind the ATP, where the trees grow thicker, just the right spot, basically. Anyway, boars recently made a lair for themselves there. And it turns out we just can't get to the stash easily now. Help out - shoot the boars, and I'll make it worth your while, for myself and my friend.\nJust need to make it in a couple of hours, because one little thing from that stash might not be needed by me anymore.\nIf you manage it, I'll respect you... And my respect, as a rule, can translate into something useful:
Need to wipe out the flesh completely Wolf is asking to clear the former farm of flesh before a group of rookies arrives there to rest. Need to manage to get rid of the mutants in eight hours. Even though this is the edge of the Zone, it's rough for rookies here. Bandits are only half the trouble, at least they understand Russian. But mutants - you can't negotiate with them. The pigs fancied a lair for themselves at the abandoned farm, and that's the only good spot for a transit camp on that side of the embankment. If you can clear the farm of the scum - I owe you. Just keep in mind, I heard a group of goofballs making plans to set up a camp there after a trek past the embankment for artifacts. I had a really urgent call of nature right then, didn't have time to warn them that they shouldn't make camp at the farm. By my estimates they'll get there in about eight hours.\nIf you manage to clear the farm in that time, you'll get this from me:
Kill the dogs at the checkpoint A pack of dogs has settled near the southern checkpoint of the Duty members in the Bar. The guard commander is asking to clear out their lair in an hour. The urgency is explained by the fact that a certain important courier is about to arrive at any minute. Despite our efforts, some monsters still break through from the Garbage side. And you've probably seen the beasts south of the checkpoint yourself. Now dogs have settled there, and we're expecting the arrival of an important courier any minute now. We don't have the right to leave the post, and there's no one else to assign this to - all the fighters are on missions... If you help kill the creatures - you'll get a reward.\nIf you get it done in an hour, then you'll get this from me:
Dynamite for Wolf from the Duty members' leadership Wolf needs dynamite for some urgent and important business. Need to manage to deliver the crate in half a day. So here's the deal, Marked One. I don't think I need to explain who Wolf is to you? He's busy with a job right now, an important one for us included. He urgently needed dynamite. Anyway, here's the crate, will you take the job to deliver it quickly? It's necessary that it absolutely ends up with Wolf no later than in half a day, otherwise... In short, the whole plan will go up in smoke otherwise.\nIf you handle it, Wolf promises a good reward:
Artifact from the Duty members' leadership for the scientists at Yantar Need to quickly, preferably by tomorrow, pass an artifact to Sakharov. Aren't you heading to Yantar? Need to quickly deliver an artifact to Sakharov, and I'll only be able to get there in a few days... Well? Sakharov said he won't cheat the courier...
Supplies for Skull's group from the Duty members' leadership Need to deliver provisions to a separate quad of Duty members, under the leadership of Skull, if possible, within two days... A group of our Duty members under the command of Major Cherepanov, callsign - Skull, is currently carrying out a special mission... Planning some actions... But... A hungry fighter is a bad fighter! We need to deliver provisions to them as quickly as possible... Will you take on this job?
Artifact from the Duty members' leadership for Raven Deliver an unusual artifact to Raven in the Dark Valley... Must manage it today. Have you met Raven in the Dark Valley? He's set up some decent channels past the Cordon... Ships all sorts of oddities out of the Zone for good money... I contacted him here, want to pass him an unusual artifact. Will you take it? Just be quick about it, one foot here, the other... Also here...
Ammo for Boryan from the Duty members' leadership Deliver AK ammo to Boryan in the Swamps... Twenty packs, armor-piercing. In a couple of days. There's a folk healer sitting in the Swamps, Boryan is his name, gathers all sorts of different, useful herbs. We want to pass him a little ammo from us here, for the peace of mind of his treks. Will you pass it on? Twenty packs. Except I promised it quickly, can you make it by tomorrow? Otherwise I'll find someone else...
Get the drugs The Duty members need "Cat's Eye" drugs, no less than 40 packs. These drugs can be obtained from the scientists in the Swamps. Recent events have shown that we haven't sufficiently worked out the issue of combat effectiveness in the dark. A decision was made to procure a few more batches of a new drug for vision enhancement. This drug cannot be supplied by our investors yet. Therefore we'll have to get it locally. Do you agree to participate? A minimum batch of forty packs is required. As far as I know, a group of scientists in the swamp have this drug. Just, let's be clear right away, there are time limits. But, if you make it in time, you'll get a certain reward. Stalkers! Cat's Eye is no longer needed. The necessary amount has been received. Attention! Vision drugs are no longer needed. The required limit has been reached.
Get illumination grenades The Duty members need illumination grenades - no less than 20 pieces. These grenades can be purchased from the scientists. Recent events have shown that we haven't sufficiently worked out the issue of combat effectiveness in the dark. A decision was made to procure illumination grenades for our quads. Do you agree to participate? We urgently need to equip one quad with twenty grenades right now. As far as I know, a group of scientists in the Swamps can offer such grenades. Just, let's be clear right away, there are time limits. But, if you make it in time, you'll get a certain reward. Stalkers! Illumination grenades are no longer needed. The necessary amount has been received. Attention! Grenades for illumination are no longer needed. The required limit has been reached.
Get flamethrowers The Duty members are interested in flamethrowers. Need to get two for starters, ask Sidorovich. Deadline is three days. Lately a dramatic situation has been developing at our checkpoints. The scum that crashes down wave after wave threatens to sweep away our defenders. What's most unpleasant - it's mostly small creatures pushing through - rats, tushkanos. Meaning the kind that are very hard to hit with traditional weaponry. We need flamethrowers! Would you agree to get a couple for starters, for a trial? The trader at the Cordon can get them through his channels. We had a deal with him a long time ago. Just let's agree on this: you don't drag your feet with this, the matter can't wait more than three days! Well, and gratitude from our side is guaranteed. Stalkers! No need to get flamethrowers anymore. The necessary amount has been received. Attention! Flamethrowers are no longer needed. We already have as many as we needed.
Get Freedom armored suits The Duty members want to get "Wind of Freedom" jumpsuits, with a gas mask. No less than two. What for - is unknown. Deadline is three days. The situation is very difficult. Decisive measures might be necessary! Our leadership needs "Wind of Freedom" jumpsuits to complete an operation. A couple of sets are needed. With a gas mask, of course. Are you ready to get these? At the same time, advertising who or what you need this armor for is completely unnecessary! Understand? I hope you won't let us down and in about three days, the specified amount will be with us? And we'll find a way to reward such an understanding stalker. Stalkers! We don't require the jumpsuits. The necessary amount has been received. Attention! Jumpsuits with a gas mask are no longer needed. We already have as many as we needed.
Get assault rifles The Duty members want to get two G36 assault rifles. Deadline - three days. The situation is getting complicated. To complete one important operation we require Western-made assault rifles. Ideally - G36s. Can you get a couple of these guns in three days? Why specifically the G36 and why the rush - don't even ask. And keep your mouth shut regarding this request. Help out - we'll reward you fairly. Stalkers! We no longer require NATO assault rifles. A scavenger brought one. Attention! The order for NATO assault rifles is canceled. We already have as many as we needed.
Artifact from Cook for Sidorovich Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? And this needs to be done not just fast, but very fast. More precisely by evening... alright, by midnight. Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? And this needs to be done not just fast, but very fast. More precisely by evening... alright, by midnight.
Package for Sidorovich from Cook Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? Only it needs to be fast! If you're short on time, then better not even take it, because it needs to be done in a day. Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? Only it needs to be fast! If you're short on time, then better not even take it, because it needs to be done in a day.
Package for Sidorovich from Cook Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? This thing looks like it's about to fall apart while you carry it. Therefore you need to make it by tomorrow morning. Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? This thing looks like it's about to fall apart while you carry it. Therefore you need to make it by tomorrow morning.
Package for Sidorovich from Cook Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? Only it needs to be fast. The client is already beside himself with waiting, and can only accept it at a specific time, so, if you don't make it in a day, then that's it. Listen, Sidorovich ordered an artifact, well my guys hustled - got it... Now we need to find someone who can pass this artifact to the Cordon... Are you maybe heading that way? Only it needs to be fast! The client is already beside himself with waiting, and can only accept it at a specific time, so, if you don't make it in a day, then that's it.
Container from the Freedom members for Sidorovich Take a container with artifacts to Sidorovich at the Cordon... Deliver by tomorrow. I gathered a few simple arts here, need to drop them off to Sidor at the Cordon. Nothing special in there, but I coded the lock... to be safe... Well? Do we have a deal?
Crate with samples for Sakharov from the Freedom members A crate with monster parts needs to be taken to Sakharov at Yantar very quickly. A couple of days is the deadline, or they'll go bad. Sakharov asked to pass him parts from various mutants... We packed a little crate here, but they stink terribly. Will you take it? Just make it quick, before they rot completely...
"Weed" for the folks in Limansk Need to take a bag of "weed" to Limansk. Mikhalych supposedly won't cheat me... Need it by tomorrow. That "plan" you dragged in, the drifters seemed to like it... Our guys blabbed about it everywhere, so now Mikhalych, a dealer from Limansk, is asking to supply him with some weed too. Will you take it? He won't cheat you. And don't trip out - there's no narc control there, but... Keep your eyes peeled.
Artifact from the Freedom members for Raven Pass an artifact to Raven... Preferably faster, need to make it by tomorrow. Came across an incomprehensible artifact here... Doesn't seem like any of the locals really need it. Try showing it to Raven. He pushes all sorts of stuff from the Zone overseas. Maybe he'll push this one too...
Mutant parts from the Freedom members for Boryan Pass mutant parts to Boryan in the Swamps... Preferably faster. Need to get it done in a couple of days. Have you heard about Boryan? Well this guy, a healer. Sits in the Swamps, gathers all sorts of different herbs, dries them. Helps the folks with illnesses. He asked to gather chimera claws, so we hustled - gathered them. Will you take it? Just needs to be quick - while they're fresh! Think you can make the round trip in a couple of days? If you're not sure - it's better we make a deal with someone else
Get the Duty members' harness For one shady business it's necessary to get two Duty armors with respiratory protection of the PSZ-9d-2 type. Deadline is three days. There's a special assignment. Not an easy one, by the way... Need the harness of the Duty members. Their armors with respiratory protection, type PSZ-9d-2. A couple of pieces. And, I hope, you understand that you shouldn't run your mouth about this request of mine? You, as they say, help me, well, and I help you... I already asked a couple of stalker acquaintances about this, but, I believe that it's you who will get my material gratitude! Stalkers! Armors are no longer needed. Already have them. Attention! All the suits we needed have already been delivered... You can save yourselves the trouble.
Get grenades Shinkar is asking to get forty F-1 type grenades. Only have three to four days total. Lately we've been catastrophically short on grenades. If you gather a batch of forty F-1s, then I'll have something to reward you with. Just hurry - the reward awaits the fastest stalker, not the laziest. Stalkers! The grenades have been delivered. Thank you for your efforts... Attention! I already gave out the reward for the grenades. There is nothing else.
Get Val assault rifles Shinkar needed two Val type assault rifles for some business. Promises a not-so-bad reward. But gave little time, only three to four days total. There was a skirmish recently with those, local cops - the Duty members. And well... In general, the folks and I have an idea to frame these 'righteous' ones... Now we need a weapon that can be linked to them. I have a request for you - get two Val type assault rifles. Well, and don't worry about the reward - I won't cheat you. Stalkers! The deal is off. Vals are not required. Attention! The assault rifles have been delivered. One lucky stalker got the reward. The rest - missed out.
Get 'Cat's Eyes' For night operations the Freedom members require "Cat's Eye" drugs. 40 pieces. Our boys need good drugs that increase visual acuity for night patrols... I heard that the nerds in the Swamps offer such pills. "Cat's Eye" they call them. Get about forty of those - I'll reward you generously! Well, I hope you understand that you're not the only one I'm asking - the first stalker to bring the 'Cat's Eyes' gets everything... Stalkers! Stocked up on 'Cat's Eyes'. Thank you very much. Attention! The reward for the 'Cat's Eyes' has already been given out. Everyone else can stop worrying.
Get illumination grenades For night operations the Freedom members require illumination grenades. 40 pieces. Our boys need illumination grenades for night patrols... I heard that the nerds in the Swamps offer such things. Get about forty of these grenades - I'll reward you generously! Well, I hope you understand that you're not the only one I'm asking - the first stalker to bring them gets everything... Stalkers! Illumination grenades are no longer needed. The necessary amount has been received. Attention! I already gave out the reward for the grenades. Not needed anymore for now.
Grenade launcher for Cap, at the Barrier, from Screw Take a grenade launcher to the Barrier, to Cap. Within an hour. If you ain't afraid of poking around the Zone, grab an RPG for the Barrier, I tuned it up a little, seems there shouldn't be any misfires, if you hold it tenderly... Just make it quick... otherwise there'll be no one to shoot it.
Scope for Nimble from Screw Pass a scope to Nimble. Needs to be delivered today. One stalker was looking for a decent scope over the network... Basically I have one... Will you pass it on? Nimble is his name, was hanging around the Bar seemingly... It should be with him today. Deal?
Armor to the Barrier from Screw Take armor to the Barrier. Needs to be done in an hour, maybe faster. I patched up an armor for one of our guys at the Barrier here. Could you take it over, make it snappy? Promised it yesterday even, but went on a bit of a bender... Only remembered by morning... Awkward...
Artifact for Baldy from Screw Take an artifact to Baldy... so it's there today. A card debt. Listen, you uh... just don't blab to our guys too much... Basically there's a dude in the Bar, Baldy. Dropped in there the other day... incognito, had some business with the local master. Had a drink to meeting up, a bite to eat, and here's this swindler. Well and I got into this bastard for an N-amount. Didn't have any cash on me, and a card debt - you know yourself. Take the artifact as payment, alright? Getting seen there unnecessarily is risky for me. Just don't drag it out.. And do it so there's no unnecessary noise...
Armor from the Barrier for Screw Need to take a Monolith members' armor to Screw... fast. We took an armor off a Monolith member here. Looks like nothing special, but it's unclear why they're so invulnerable in them! Take it to our handyman - Screw, maybe he'll find something in it... Think you can run there in an hour, even with two smoke breaks. What about you? Won't turn it down?
Gift for the Hunter in the Bar from Cap If I see the hunter, I'll tell him there are no more bloodsuckers in the village at the warehouses. Need to inform him within two days, before he leaves and the mandible goes bad. There was a Stalker here recently. Everyone calls him Hunter. Seemingly hunts mutants and turns in their parts on the mainland. He was really beating himself up that bloodsuckers in the little village, right near here, roughed him up badly. Kept asking us to deal with them if we got the chance, even left arts - as an advance. In short, if you meet him - tell him we sorted out those creatures! Here's a trophy, a tentacle from one of their posse! Well, will you make the victim happy?
Mutant Parts for Sakharov from Cap Deliver two electrochimera kidneys to Sakharov at Yantar. Must be done within 24 hours. Sakharov put out a message over the network saying that he and the other eggheads holed up at Yantar are after parts from rare mutants. As it happens, a couple of those showed up at our checkpoint recently — electric chimeras, you've heard of them, right? Nasty beasts. We barely held them off, and even then we took losses. Anyway, take what's left of those things to Yantar and let them study it. Maybe they'll figure out some kind of countermeasure...\nJust don't drag your feet — try to get it done within a day, or the innards will rot.
Artifact for Mikhalych from Cap Need to take a unique artifact to Limansk, to Mikhalych. Supposedly promise a good reward. I can manage it in a couple of days. You know Mikhalych - the trader in Limansk? He's interested in all sorts of unusual artifacts. And here I just came across one. Won't take the job to carry it? Even though Mikhalych is kin to Sidor, he's not such a tightwad... won't shortchange the messenger.
PDA for Petrenko from Skull Pass a Freedom member's PDA to Petrenko. Deadline - two days. We had a little spat with the Freedom members here, so we peeked at an interesting little file in the PDA of one of them, who's currently having a frying pan heated for him by devils... Couldn't decrypt it right off... Will you take the job to quickly pass it to Petrenko? I sense there's some interesting info in there. Just make it snappy, before it gets outdated!
Little device for Prapor from Skull Take a little device to Prapor at the Garbage checkpoint. Deadline - two days. Know the commander of our checkpoint at the Garbage? He asked to find a certain little device, so we confiscated one from a layabout. What the hell do fools need good devices for... In short, if you carry it there - you'll get gratitude from Prapor personally.
Walkie-talkie for Kitsenko from Skull Pass a walkie-talkie to Sergeant Kitsenko in the Bar. Deadline - two days. Know Kitsenko? He commands our quad on the southern edge of the Bar... Anyway, I have a proposal for him that I don't want to trust to regular comm channels. Will you take the walkie-talkie to him? I encrypted one channel there... He'll reward you, don't doubt it...
Assault rifle for Brom from Skull Take an assault rifle to the perpetually drunk Duty member Brom. Deadline - two days. One of our guys... Brom... Got into big trouble - lost his weapon. We clashed with those stoned freaks here and took what seems like a similar gun off one of them! Will you take it to the Bar? I think Brom will be on cloud nine, just tell him not to be in a particular hurry to get there, there's enough work for him here too!
Artifact for Sidorovich from Prapor Take an artifact to Sidorovich. Need to make it by tomorrow. Sidorovich asked to bring him odd artifacts, if we come across any... Here, we found one of those... Will you take it? Just make it quick. He's waiting. Today even.
Hooves for the scientists from Prapor Take ten special hooves to the Swamps, to the scientists. Need to make it by tomorrow. Know about the nerds in the Swamps? They're studying the little beasts intensely... We gathered some boar hooves here, the kind they're interested in. Will you take them quickly, before they rot? By tomorrow? If you're not heading that way, then I'll just send one of my guys.
Tails for the hunters from Prapor Take twenty fresh blind dog tails to the Gut, to the hunters. Need to make it by tomorrow. The hunters in the Gut make all sorts of things from tails. Well we gathered some choice ones for them. Will you take them quickly, before they rot? By tomorrow? If you're not heading that way, then I'll just send one of my guys.
Data for the scientists from Prapor Take memory cards from recording devices to the scientists at Yantar. Must deliver in two days. The nerds who hang out at Yantar set up their devices here, and well, asked to send the results from them sometimes. Only needs to be done quickly, because data gets outdated fast. If you're not heading that way, then I'll just send someone from my guys.
Artifact for Sidorovich from Mikhalych Take a unique artifact to Sidorovich, at the Cordon. Need to make it in two days. Look ye 'ere, what I've got, eh?! Like it?! This thing needs takin' to my crony - at the Cordon. That old spec... negotiator knows better what to do with it. Just don't sleep on the road, fly like the wind, 'cause it's unstable, this formation.. got it? So it don't turn out that - carried an artifact, but brought a handful of zilch.
Artifact for the scientists from Mikhalych Take an unknown artifact to Yantar. Maybe it'll interest the scientists. Need to get it done in a day. My lads brought me somethin' incomprehensible... I ain't ever seen such a thing, and keepin' it on me - is somewhat frightenin', and to throw it out... *cough*.. well the toad would choke me with greed... So I already contacted who I need to - can ye find the way to Yantar? Oh, then take it. The men of science said they'll give the courier a good reward, ye'll be glad.
Supplies for the Freedom members from Mikhalych The trader from Limansk is passing some food supplies to the Freedom members at the Army Warehouses. Maybe bloodsucker tentacles in tomato sauce? One day deadline, or they'll go sour. Here's the deal - the connoisseurs of local cuisine, who dwell at the Warehouses, asked me real nice to pass 'em some human grub... 'cause seemingly "bloodsucker maws in tomato sauce" just won't go down their gullets anymore... heh-heh-heh. Generally I've got somethin' more or less ordinary for 'em... the kind everyone eats. Just be swift about it, 'cause ye know yerself - don't eat for a day here, and ye've croaked from starvation... there'll be no one to settle up with ye.
Armor to the Barrier from Mikhalych Take a new armor to Cap, maybe it'll protect against this unknown weapon. Gave a deadline until tomorrow. There's an uncle at the Barrier - Cap... heard of him? Maybe he is a captain, a sea wolf, and maybe not - that ain't important... He and I got our own, little commerce. So look. He said that lately, these... Monolith members are pushin' on 'em, or whatever they are, and they got some new weapon too... Gotta take this armor to him - maybe it'll help him against those adversaries, that push from the Radar... Will ye take it? He'll give ye somethin' for it - somethin' good, don't doubt it.
Artifact for Boryan from Mikhalych Take a new artifact to Boryan, in the Swamps. Need to run there within three days. Ye heard 'bout Boryan? Well that one, supposedly the "folk doctor" - sits in the Swamps, gathers some herbs, dries 'em... helps good folks with all sorts of ailments and sicknesses - a good man. And me and the uncles here decided, that we gotta thank him too, from our side, somehow: gift him an artifact like this, that's got healin' properties. Just gotta pull this off snappy - so that magical power don't vanish from the artifact! Can ye do it in three days? If ye can, then Boryan will pour ye a bit of somethin' useful - he's got some, for stalkers. And if not, then I'll look for somebody else..
"Gift" for Tooth from Borzyi Pass a crate to Tooth... Ye heard 'bout Tooth - well he's at the Bar, sellin' all sorts of tall tales to the suckers for serious dough - heard of him? Yeah I know, he conned you too.. heh-heh. So here's the deal, we gathered somethin' for him here... basically you ain't interested in this. I jotted down a couple lines for him there. He'll basically understand what it's 'bout... Will ya zip over there? Gotta get it done in half a day. Tooth will throw somethin' your way, don't doubt it - you won't be left holdin' the bag. Well, did we chop it up?
Package for Vakhtang from Borzyi Need to pass a package from Borzyi to the merc Vakhtang, at the Army Warehouses. So like here's the deal - completely, fo' real, by chance one of our bros came across all sorts of busted PDAs. A pretty serious pile. And one dude at the Warehouses, Vakhtang is his street name, collects 'em. I packed 'em in a little crate, well you uh, haul it over. If in half a day, then Vakhtang will pour ya somethin'. Alright?
Find out what happened to the courier. Need to find out where the courier disappeared to and, if possible, find his belongings. In short, here's the deal - three clowns were hoofin' it to me, one had some serious intel. I told 'em, shit, keep your heads down, you seals, take the back roads to me, so no one even catches a shadow... But no - got into a scrap with some stalker trash on the way, only one made it out of that mess. Another one supposedly got nailed on the spot with a grenade, and the third seems like he got wounded, maybe ain't kicked the bucket yet. And here word got 'round that the Duty members pinched someone, someone from our guys.. If it's my courier, that's gonna be real bad - the Duty members got this one beast, Ivantsov is his last name - absolutely zero street smarts, a cop's mug... he'll crack anyone you want in a second.\nYou should go, rub shoulders there, cipher out what's what, who they pinched... Only the first guy you meet ain't gonna start waggin' his tongue there, I reckon... Roll up on one Duty member, tip him off that you're from me. In short, find out - who they pinched and what they found on him, and if they found anything at all... A notebook, a note, a journal... a message, in short, somethin'... And if you bring it to me, you'll be a real beauty - I'll roll out a prize, everyone will be green with envy. And don't drag it out - two days deadline for this, no more.
What are you lookin' out for here? An artifact? For what? I'll find you a dozen broken PDAs like that. I'll go to that stalker of yours... Hmm, and who are you? Well? What you want? You had a skirmish with bandits here recently. Seems like you even shot somebody... Was there anything interesting on him? They say you can sometimes find magazines on them, and I've already re-read everything in the Bar... Nah... Nothin', all sorts of trash: a couple of assault rifles - off the corpse and the half-dead guy - we didn't even bother finishin' him off. And a few bullets... No magazines. Basically, if we hadn't found a couple bottles of booze on 'em, there wouldn't have been anythin' interestin' at all. Well, also found a PDA on the corpse, two bullets hit it right on, in short - complete junk, it's yours... Yeah... just a casing, you could say, and a bent and beaten one at that... Well, since there's nothing interesting, I'll go re-read old magazines in the Bar.
I visited the Duty member. Says their prisoner was barely breathing - so they whacked him, why drag a half-dead guy around. Whacked him... That's good, though it's bad too. Well, what 'bout his gear? What gear, there was nothing - either he lost it while crawling, or destroyed it, or something else. Here, just a broken PDA... Yeah... This ain't a PDA, you can't even tell whose it was now. Well then... there was nothin' - and that's good too. Means it wasn't him, or he managed to figure it out and got rid of it in time... Thanks for that anyway.
Flesh hides for the hunters from Jaeger Pass five flesh hides to Ignat in the Gut from Jaeger. Two days deadline. I shot some pigs here, gathered some hides. Now I need to pass them to the hunters in the Gut for tanning. You wouldn't happen to be heading that way? Just think it over right away: the hides will go bad if you don't make it in a couple of days. If anything - I'll hire the guys from Freedom.
Monster parts for Boryan from Jaeger Jaeger is passing a crate with monster meat to Boryan in the Swamps. Two days deadline. I want to make Boryan, the healer from the Swamps, happy - his herbs helped, thanks to him... Here, I gathered something for his medical practice, want to pass it to him. Are you heading towards the Swamps by chance? Need to carry it over in a couple of days, make it before they go bad. Will you take the job?
Chimera claws for Pakhom from Jaeger Pass five chimera claws to Pakhom, the quartermaster of the zoologists' base in the Swamps, for experiments. Gave a couple of days deadline. The nerds who organized a base for themselves in the Swamps were very interested in chimera claws for their experiments. If you're heading that way, would you agree to quickly pass along five of these claws? Just try to make the trip in a couple of days? Seems like they need particularly fresh claws, trash is not required.
Artifacts to Yantar from Jaeger Pass two unusual artifacts to Sakharov. Must manage it by tomorrow. I accidentally came across a couple of unusual arts here, in our thicket. If you have the chance to take them to Sakharov at Yantar, you'll come out ahead. Just make it quick, preferably by tomorrow.
Tails for Sidorovich from Jaeger Pass ten pseudo tails to Sidorovich. Two days deadline. Sidorovich gave me a signal that he urgently needs pseudo tails. I shot some here. Very good ones. You weren't planning on heading towards the Cordon? Maybe you'll grab them? Need to manage it in a couple of days here, so they don't rot...
Batch of sausages for the Freedom members Pass a box with a batch of sausages from the hunters to Lukash at the Warehouses. Two days deadline, or they'll stink anyway. By agreement with the guys from Freedom, we are obligated to supply a batch of sausages of our own making every week. The deadline is approaching, and there's no one to pass them with - will you take the job to deliver the box with our "delicacies" to Lukash? He'll settle up with you - don't worry.
Batch of sausages for the Duty members Need to pass another batch of "delicacies" from the hunters to Petrenko... Two days deadline, or they'll stink anyway. We made a deal with the Duty members. Here, from time to time we pass them a batch of sausages of our own making. There hasn't been a courier from them in the last few days. Will you take the job to deliver the box? It's not a lot, a weekly supply. And Petrenko will settle up with you fairly.
Sausage from the hunters for Boryan Take a box with sausage to Boryan, in the Swamps, out of gratitude. Must make it in a day. Boryan, the doctor in the Swamps, helps out from time to time with his herbs for ailments. Well, and out of gratitude we pass our delicacies to him from time to time. If you're heading there, would you agree to grab a little box with sausages? Just try to make the trip quickly - Boryan said that mutants devoured all his grub when he was away. Can you make it in a day?
Batch of sausages for the bros in the Dark Valley Need to pass another batch of "delicacies" from the hunters to Borov, in the Dark Valley... Must make it in a day. We have a small deal with the guys from the Dark Valley. We give them our sausages, they also help out with a few things. Won't you take the job to carry over another batch? Just make it quick - Borov doesn't like delays. If you think you won't make it there in a day - I'll just send one of my guys.
Ammo for Jaeger in the Red Forest Pass two hundred rounds with a heavy unjacketed bullet to Jaeger in the Red Forest within two days. Jaeger from the Red Forest sometimes tosses us parts of the beasts from over there. Recently asked to gather some ammo for a shotgun with a heavy bullet for him. Won't you pass them from us? Can you make it there in two days? If not, then we'll send someone else...
Destroy the flesh lair Ignat is asking to clear out the flesh lair in the Gut. Promises to throw in an artifact and sausages as a reward, but need to manage to finish the mutants off in a day. There's a certain matter here. The local ecologists got the urge to run some experiment. And as luck would have it, fleshes fancied that area. My guys are busy right now, and the nerds are burning up, that's how urgent it is. Will you take the job to help science and rid them of the neighborhood with mutants? Need to get it done in a day. \nI'll make it worth your while. I have a little artifact and I'll toss in some of our sausage.
Medicine from Boryan for the Duty members Take a package with medicinal herbs to the Bar, to Colonel Petrenko. One day deadline. The Duty members asked to pass along another batch of my herbs. Would you agree? Just make it quick, seems one of them over there has a bad stomach - he's dying. Can you manage it in a day? Petrenko will reward you. If you're not sure - it's better I ask someone else.
Medicine from Boryan for the hunters Take a package with medicinal herbs to the Gut, to the sawmill. One day deadline. The hunters asked to pass along another batch of my herbs. Would you agree? Just make it quick, seems one of them over there has a bad stomach - he's dying. Can you manage it in a day? Ignat will reward you. If you're not sure - it's better I ask someone else.
Medicine from Boryan for the Freedom members Find Shinkar at the Freedom members' base and pass him a package with medicinal herbs. Only a day deadline. There was a courier from the Freedom members recently. Shinkar complains that an illness has befallen them - asked to pass another batch of my herbs. Would you agree? Just make it quick. Can you manage it in a day? Shinkar will pour you a bit of something. If you're not sure - it's better I ask someone else.
Medicine from Boryan to Limansk Take a couple of packages with medicinal herbs to Limansk, to Mikhalych. Need to deliver in a day, day and a half. Mikhalych sent a little man over, seems like my herbs are doing well with the stalkers there. Asked to pass another batch of my herbs. Would you agree? Just make it quick, seems one of them over there has a bad stomach - he's dying. Can you manage it in a day? Mikhalych will find a way to reward the courier. But if you're not sure - it's better I ask someone else.
Medicine from Boryan for the bros in the Dark Valley Take a few packages with medicinal herbs to the Valley, to Borov. Half a day for delivery. Know about Borov? He's the main boss in the Dark Valley. An errand boy of his came running, asking to send more herbs. Would you agree? Just make it quick, something's burning up with them over there. Can you manage it in about 12 hours? Borov will reward you. If you're not sure - it's better I ask someone else.
Medicine from Boryan for Jaeger in the Red Forest Take a couple of packages with medicinal herbs to Jaeger in the Red Forest. Need to manage it in a day and a half maximum. Haven't met with Jaeger in the Red Forest? He manages the mutant herds over there... Sometimes sends me some guts too, needed for medical practice. Will you take a couple of packages with my herbs to him? Just see here, the package needs to be delivered in about a day and a half - max. If you have your own business somewhere, then I'll ask somebody else.
Cloak for Boryan from Borov Take a cloak made of flesh hide to the Swamps, to Boryan. Need to deliver within two days. Boryan, the homegrown doc who hangs out in the Swamps, helped our sick bros with his roots. Take our signature little cloak to him out of gratitude. Just do it real snappy, 'cause seems like he gets offended that we don't hook him up on time. Think you can manage it in a couple of days? No? If you ain't sure - better I send somebody else...
Cloak for the hunters from Borov Take a cloak made of flesh hide to the Gut, to the sawmill, within a day. The guys from the Gut send us gifts from time to time, so out of gratitude I'd like to pass along our signature little cloak. Will ya fly over there, to Ignat? Just make it quick - one foot here, and the other gets torn off... Ha-ha. Joke. Basically gotta make it in a day! You takin' the job?
PDA for Vakhtang from Borov Take 25 broken PDAs to the Army Warehouses, to the merc Vakhtang. Deadline - two days. Found some broken PDAs here by chance. And there's a little man who's very interested in 'em. Sits at the Army Warehouses, Vakhtang. If you take the job to drag this scrap over to him, he'll reward ya. Just make it quick - two days for all the screw-ups. Think you can handle it?
Carbines for Vakhtang from Borov Take two TDI KRISS Vector submachine guns to the Army Warehouses to the merc Vakhtang. Deadline - two days. There's a man sittin' at the Army Warehouses, goes by Vakhtang. If you take the job to drag a couple of carbines over to him, he'll reward ya. What he needs 'em for - I don't know. But, apparently, he needs 'em bad, since he demands it gets done in two days max. Think you can handle it?
Sidor, I uh... Well, what do you want, invalid? Medicine and ammo - that's good, of course, thanks for throwing them my way, but... how about some cash or something more substantial, eh? I'll work it off. I'm tired of walking around like a ragamuffin... There is one little job, just right for ragamuffins. I'm joking, joking.\nYou already know about the beast-catchers in the Gut, right? I don't give a damn about their hunting business - let them salt whatever guts they want, that's not the point. The point is that they somehow ship all these offal through their own channels. And I'm very interested, are artifacts also leaving through those same channels? The situation is such that I don't like how my main buyer has been behaving lately... Apparently, I'll have to look for another, more generous one. So look, Marked One, talk to the hunters. Their bosses won't say anything, that's understandable, but maybe one of the rank-and-file trappers knows how they smuggle the loot past the perimeter. In short, clear up this point. I have a feeling I didn't run around for sausage just for nothing, either. You sly old bug. I have an acquaintance, a hunter I can talk to about this topic. Nazar is his name. But what if he gets stubborn? I have a feeling I didn't run around for sausage just for nothing, either. You sly old bug. And who am I supposed to ask around there? Every random hunter I meet? Sorry, Sidorovich, but I'm forced to decline. I didn't like wandering around the Gut. A very unwelcoming place. Maybe later. Well you try your best, show some eloquence. And try not to drag it out. Commerce doesn't like downtime. If you don't manage it in a couple of days, then I'll find another messenger, a more nimble one. Why, every one? I have one accommodating guy in mind, drops by here sometimes. Nazar is his name, he has a camp in the southern part of the Gut near the power line mast. Teaches the young ones the hunting trade there. Have a chat with him. And try not to drag it out. Commerce doesn't like downtime. If you don't manage it in a couple of days, then I'll find another messenger, a more nimble one. We have a deal. Prepare the reward.
Can I distract you for a word or two? Yeah no problem, let's chat. I'm thinking, maybe I should become a hunter after all. From what I see, you've set yourselves up pretty nicely, right? Does the profit justify such a life? How should I put it... It's better than slaving away in a mine for pennies. And the risk is almost the same. I think you won't lose out, friend Marked One, if you join up with us, if you have a firm character. In our line of work the main thing is not to rush, not to fuss, then there will be a profit, and your skin will remain intact. I think this suits me. And who buys your catch? Is it one person or what? Why I'm asking is, what if the buyer-supplier thread snaps and we're left with nothing... You're a calculating one, I see. Let's do this: I'll put your mind at ease on this account, if you help me out with a job. Sometimes we have particularly lucky days, when the catch is significantly higher than the "hospital average". So as not to drive down the price of the goods, we've made it a rule to set aside the surplus in prepared stashes and turn them in the next day or even later. Walk through three places, take everything from the stashes and bring it to me, then we'll return to the topic you're interested in. How are you, orient yourself well with a map? So you don't wander around for a long time searching, I'll put some marks in your PDA. And keep in mind, Marked One, we will still check these stashes whether today or tomorrow, even if you refuse to help me now. Therefore, if you don't return quickly with the trophies, we will assume that you pocketed them yourself. Fair? Sounds like you're twisting my arm. Well I have to test the seriousness of your intentions. You come around here, asking strange questions. A person could think all sorts of things... not good ones.
I gathered all the goods from your stashes. Attaboy! I see you really want to become a hunter. Come on, put it all into my bag. Yeah. Now how about dispelling my doubts regarding your sales channels? A deal is a deal. Our slaughterhouse is tied to a man who is very well-known in narrow circles, the right hand of some oligarch. He handles major funds, as you understand. Has several of his own plants and factories all over the world. They produce medicine, something else related to chemistry, conduct accompanying research. It's for these latter purposes that he needs our goods. Wants, apparently, to rake in even more dough on new drugs, and then buy himself the presidency in some African country or something like that. In general, the road here is very well-paved. It won't break, rest assured. We don't mess with arts and other anomalies, because you can go to jail. That's strictly monitored. Without specific connections with the enforcers you shouldn't even start. But no one pays much attention to tails and hooves. Understood. Well, you've reassured me, thanks.
